content:
I'm working on Map Tile Event dialogs. A vast majority of these use Args as placeholders in the text; i.e. `"(RelicConfig.Arg1)"` / `"(Skill.Arg1)"` / `Gain Arg1 Black Sigils`

When there are multiple Args in the same line of text, they're always increased in sequential order;
`Infect "(Skill.Arg1)", a random card gains Orison: "(EnchantConfig.Arg2)"`
I'll be using this line as an example, as it's the first time I've had to tackle this. I'm trying to avoid putting Symptom text ahead of any beneficial effects, so I need to reverse the order of this line.

My question is, which is more important in the code;
- (1) That Args always stay in sequential order regardless of how I reorder the text?
  - `Engrave the Orison "(EnchantConfig.Arg1)" on a random Command card, but develop "(Skill.Arg2)".`
or:
- (2) That Args always stay in their original state regardless of where they're placed?
  - `Engrave the Orison "(EnchantConfig.Arg2)" on a random Command card, but develop "(Skill.Arg1)".`

My assumption is (2), as I've seen `Arg1` thrown raw into a sentence; my theory would be proven if I could find a sentence like `Gain Arg1 Black Sigils and restore Arg2 HP`, but I haven't seen anything like that yet, so I'd like to check and make sure. Thanks!
(2) is correct. some of Korean texts are already in different order (because Korean uses S-O-V order)
ive seen ingame texts working correct

Okay, cool. I'm also curious about another two things while I'm on this topic, if you or anyone else knows.
I'm assuming it's just kinda normal and plenty functional for a line to be missing an Arg1 and skip directly to higher Args? `[Dispel the Illusion] Lose (Arg3) HP.` 
And more importantly, what does [ExDesc(X)] mean?
i believe ExDesc is some kind of conditional texts

that's what I figured, I remember seeing Nourishing Mother's description change in those places after Resonance, but I'm not 100% sure what those tags are doing in Junction. Maybe it's a R Wheel thing I've never used or noticed, i dunno

Hello. I decided to sit down and translate the characters' messages and came across a problem when the characters address the main character as "Keeper". The problem is that in the Russian language we have different endings for almost every word depending on the gender of the person/animal. And in the case of the keeper, too. In some messages there is a variation of different versions of the main character, but this is only between Mr. and Ms., and not everywhere. Is it possible to somehow play around with this moment and add such a thing to the text itself, if it was not originally intended? I'm talking about "Male=Mr.,Female=Mrs.,"

yes, you can use the gender tag:
```{Male=TEXT1,Female=TEXT2}```
in this case TEXT1 will be displayed for male Keepers and TEXT2 for females
Thank you, Lens♥️ i was afraid to put it
